task: The chef sliced the bread for the table. It was still warm from the oven and smelled wonderful.
Nathaniel showed Abraham the antique map. He had purchased it at an auction the previous month.
The wind shook the branches of the old oak. It scattered leaves across the entire backyard.
implementations: The pronoun "It" refers to "the old oak".

task: Bridget visited Madeline in the village. She had not seen her old friend in over five years.
The phone rang on the kitchen counter. It interrupted the family during their evening meal together.
implementations: The pronoun "It" refers to "the kitchen counter".

task: Damian apologized to Fernando for the misunderstanding. He had spoken without checking the facts carefully first.
The river carried the boat downstream gently. It eventually reached the small fishing village by evening.
Marigold introduced Lavender to her parents. She had been dating her for nearly a year by then.
The bell tower stood prominently in the village square. It had been built nearly four hundred years ago.
implementations: The pronoun "It" refers to "the bell tower".
The pronoun "It" refers to "the village square".
